default_creds.context: Default Postgres (pgvector/pgvector:pg16) creds in docker-compose.run.yml: POSTGRES_USER=morphik / POSTGRES_PASSWORD=morphik, port 5432 published to host

JWT_SECRET_KEY fallback in docker-compose.run.yml (JWT_SECRET_KEY=${JWT_SECRET_KEY:-your-secret-key-here}); core/config.py uses 'dev-secret-key' fallback in bypass mode. Predictable HS256 secret = forgeable tokens if auth is later enabled without changing it

install_tell: curl -sSL https://install.morphik.ai | bash   (downloads docker-compose.run.yml + morphik.toml, runs ghcr.io/morphik-org/morphik-core:latest). Alternative: git clone https://github.com/morphik-org/morphik-core && docker compose up
misconfig_patterns: Quickstart accepted with defaults (Enter at LOCAL_URI_PASSWORD prompt) -> bypass_auth_mode=true -> unauth UI+API on internet-facing :8000
Default morphik.toml committed with bypass_auth_mode=true + mode=self_hosted + environment=development left unchanged in production
JWT_SECRET_KEY left at fallback your-secret-key-here / dev-secret-key (predictable, forgeable)
Postgres :5432 published to host with morphik:morphik default creds
Redis :6379 published with no auth (redis-server --appendonly yes, no requirepass)
Telemetry/heartbeat left enabled, beaconing installation_id + version to logs.morphik.ai

passive: http.favicon.hash: (Morphik Console Next.js UI on :3003 - derive from live favicon, not yet cataloged)

response_markers: GET /openapi.json -> paths include /retrieve/chunks/grouped AND /local/generate_uri AND /ingest/file (this triad is Morphik-unique)
GET /health -> JSON with component checks for postgres, redis, pgvector, colpali
unauth POST /documents (empty filter) returns 200 with a JSON document list when bypass_auth_mode=true (confirms open posture without exfiltrating content)
active_probe.false_positive_check: Do NOT fingerprint on the OpenAPI/Swagger title: core/api.py instantiates `app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)` with NO title argument, so /openapi.json returns the FastAPI DEFAULT `"title":"FastAPI"` and /docs shows the generic "FastAPI - Swagger UI" — this collides with tens of thousands of unrelated FastAPI apps and is worthless as a Morphik tell. Likewise /ping's `{"status":"ok"}` and a bare 8000 port are generic. Confirm Morphik specifically by the vendor-unique ROUTE SET in /openapi.json: the co-occurrence of `/retrieve/chunks/grouped` + `/local/generate_uri` + `/ingest/file` (ColPali multimodal RAG routes) appears together only in Morphik. A second corroborator: the `morphik://` connection-URI scheme returned by /local/generate_uri, and the legacy telemetry tell SERVICE_NAME="databridge-core" / heartbeat to logs.morphik.ai. A host that only echoes /ping or has no /retrieve/* routes is a FastAPI false positive, not Morphik.

deployment_tells: Outbound heartbeat POST to https://logs.morphik.ai/api/heartbeat every 4h (TELEMETRY_ENABLED default true)
Legacy project name leaks in telemetry: SERVICE_NAME default 'databridge-core' (Morphik was formerly DataBridge)
Container names morphik-app / morphik-worker / morphik-redis / morphik-postgres / morphik-ollama / morphik-ui from docker-compose.run.yml
Next.js 'Morphik Console' UI on :3003 (profile-gated), API on :8000, embedding/ColPali API on :6000 (morphik_embedding_api_domain=http://localhost:6000)
arq worker queue on Redis :6379; pgvector/pgvector:pg16 on :5432
pivot_paths: GET /openapi.json -> enumerate full route surface + confirm Morphik triad without touching data
GET /ping and GET /health -> liveness + component map (postgres/redis/pgvector/colpali up) read-only
POST /documents with empty/minimal filter -> count + metadata of ingested corpus (names ARE the finding; do not pull bodies)
GET /models/available and /models -> registered LLM models + provider config (reveals which upstream keys are wired)
GET /chats -> presence + count of stored conversations (sensitivity signal) without reading transcripts
If :5432 exposed: confirm pgvector reachability via banner only (Insight: banner != schema; do not auth) for attribution that the document store is co-exposed
vulnerabilities: No published CVE or GHSA as of 2026-06-12 (github.com/morphik-org/morphik-core/security/advisories: 'There aren't any published security advisories'). The security-relevant default is design-level: bypass_auth_mode=true shipped in default morphik.toml + installer defaulting to skip-password = unauthenticated full-fleet read/write on an internet-exposed :8000.

Predictable default HS256 JWT secret (your-secret-key-here / dev-secret-key fallback) = token forgery / auth bypass even when bypass_auth_mode is later set false, if operator does not rotate JWT_SECRET_KEY. Affected: all self-host versions using the shipped docker-compose.run.yml fallback.
auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: the default `morphik.toml` shipped in morphik-org/morphik-core ships, verbatim, `[auth] jwt_algorithm = "HS256" / bypass_auth_mode = true  # Enabled by default for easier local development / dev_user_id = "dev_user"`, and `[morphik] mode = "self_hosted"`, `[service] environment = "development"`. `core/auth_utils.py` verify_token: when `settings.bypass_auth_mode` is true it returns `AuthContext(user_id=settings.dev_user_id, app_id=None)` with NO Authorization header checked, so every request (UI and REST/API) is served as the dev_user with no token. `core/config.py` loads `bypass_auth_mode` straight from the toml and only raises "JWT_SECRET_KEY is required" when bypass is OFF; `dev_mode` is a backward-compat alias property for `bypass_auth_mode`. The canonical installer `install_docker.sh` (run via `curl -sSL https://install.morphik.ai | bash`) downloads `docker-compose.run.yml` (publishes `8000:8000`) plus this `morphik.toml`, and prompts "enter a secure LOCAL_URI_PASSWORD (or press Enter to skip for local-only access)" — pressing Enter (the default, lowest-friction path) prints "No LOCAL_URI_PASSWORD provided - enabling authentication bypass (bypass_auth_mode=true)". So a user who runs the documented quickstart and accepts defaults gets a fully unauthenticated UI+API; auth exists but is OFF unless the operator sets a LOCAL_URI_PASSWORD / flips bypass_auth_mode=false. Classified configurable-default-off (it skews open-by-default in practice because the install prompt defaults to skip).
sensitive_assets: Ingested RAG corpus: raw documents, PDFs, images, parsed chunks (any private/PII/PHI/contract data the operator loaded) readable unauth via POST /documents, /retrieve/docs, /retrieve/chunks, /batch/documents
Vector store contents (pgvector chunks + ColPali multivector embeddings) and metadata filters revealing business taxonomy

Upstream LLM provider API keys: OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, ASSEMBLYAI_API_KEY, TURBOPUFFER_API_KEY, AWS creds held in env/.env (used for completion+embedding; abusable for LLMjacking if env or completion config leaks)
Knowledge-graph definitions (/graph) exposing entity/relationship structure of the private knowledge base = business logic
Conversation/chat history: GET /chats, GET /chat/{chat_id} returns prior queries and model answers
Postgres directly exposed on 5432 with morphik:morphik (vector + document_metadata tables) when host port published
App/connection tokens via /apps and /apps/rotate_token; predictable JWT secret (your-secret-key-here / dev-secret-key) enables token forgery
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
